verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is canonically a cytosolic Hsp70 chaperone with no TM or secretory signal, but it is robustly documented on the outer leaflet of stressed and tumor cells. Flow cytometry on viable tumor cells, surface biotinylation, and the cmHsp70.1 mAb show an extracellularly exposed C-terminal epitope on tumor biopsies (colorectal, lung, pancreatic, leukemic blasts) while normal tissues are surface-negative. Membrane anchoring is via direct outer-leaflet lipid binding (Gb3, externalized PS) — its own mechanism, not transient receptor recruitment. This qualifies as cell-state-induced surface display (tumor/stress). Clinical/preclinical mHsp70-targeted programs (cmHsp70.1, TPP peptide, NK-based therapy) confirm surface accessibility.
